course_title: Introduction to Research in Exercise Science
description:
Offered: Fall
This course involves the study of research methods and statistics commonly used in the Exercise Science discipline. Students will study basic statistical analyses; the tenets of the scientific method; threats to reliability and validity; how to formulate a research question and develop a testable hypothesis; and how to critically evaluate published research. Additionally, students will develop a proposal for an independent research project.
Prerequisite(s): Grade of 2.0 or higher in BIOL 1200 and EXSS 1011
Note: EXSS majors only.
